_note: DUMMY sample registry for the template. Replace channel tokens, cronJobId/launchdLabel, and jobs with your own. Channel IDs are placeholders (e.g. <CHANNEL_GENERAL>) — real deployments use the chat platform's channel IDs.

policy.sourceOfTruth: This file is the deterministic registry for ALL cron jobs (category: reminder | report | automation). Cron job names/prompts must not be inferred; this metadata is the source of truth. New cron jobs MUST be registered here at creation time.
policy.summaryRule: Weekly reminder summaries include only jobs with category=reminder, enabled=true, and weeklySummary.targets containing the requested target.
policy.privacyRule: A reminder is never summarized into a channel unless that channel is explicitly listed in weeklySummary.targets.
policy.driftRule: scripts/cron_registry_check.py compares this registry against the live scheduler weekly. Registry edits do NOT sync to cron; fix drift manually in whichever side is wrong.
policy.reviewRule: Each job has reviewBy (YYYY-MM-DD). When overdue, decide keep/change/remove, then bump reviewBy.
policy.executionRule: Each job declares agentTurn. agentTurn=false means the job is a pure relay (deterministic script stdout -> chat); it runs via the OS scheduler (runner=launchd, scripts/relay_run.py <id>) and must NOT consume an agent turn. agentTurn=true jobs need LLM judgment/generation and run via the LLM cron (runner=openclaw-cron). When creating a new periodic job, decide agentTurn first: if no LLM judgment is needed, make it a relay job.

jobs.title: Weekly memory tidy-up
jobs.purpose: Run skills/memory-maintenance to keep the L0 index thin, fix dead pointers, and distill old daily logs.

jobs.title: Morning standup nudge
jobs.purpose: Remind the owner of today's top priority and any calendar events in the next 24h.

jobs.title: Morning calendar relay
jobs.purpose: Pure relay: a deterministic script prints today's calendar; stdout is posted verbatim. No LLM judgment needed.
